codeReview.summary: The implementation fully covers all expected behavior from the feature description. Four member directories were created with correct pyproject.toml files, proper ROCm/CUDA hardware isolation via optional-dependencies, correct package naming, HF Hub transport module with upload_to_hub/download_from_hub, and 25 comprehensive smoke tests. The worker's deviation from the skill's GPU index URL approach (using a single umbrella pytorch index with optional extras + conflicts instead of per-member sub-indexes) was a justified workaround for a known uv workspace limitation. The implementation was subsequently fixed in two follow-up commits (77b8527, e04ef0e) to address the Python 3.10 compatibility issue.

issues.description: Original commit had requires-python = '>=3.11' (bumped for tomllib). This was fixed in commit 77b8527 to '>=3.10' with a tomli fallback. The fix was further refined in e04ef0e to use tomli directly. Issue is fully resolved.

The PyTorch index URL is the generic https://download.pytorch.org/whl/ which serves CPU wheels. GPU variants require manual --index-url override on the CLI. This is documented in comments and in architecture.md. It is a workaround for uv's workspace limitation and is acceptable, though users must know to pass --index-url. The services.yaml install_rocm/install_cuda commands do not include the --index-url flags, which could be misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The install_rocm and install_cuda commands in services.yaml (uv sync --extra rocm, uv sync --extra cuda) are incomplete — they install the torch dependency from the generic CPU-only pytorch index. To get actual GPU wheels, users must also pass --index-url. The services.yaml commands should either include the index URL or note this limitation. Currently the CLI comments in pyproject.toml document the full commands, but services.yaml does not.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml lines 3-4: 'install_rocm: uv sync --extra rocm' and 'install_cuda: uv sync --extra cuda' — missing --index-url flag. pyproject.toml lines 40-41 document the correct full commands with --index-url.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that uv workspaces cannot have conflicting package indexes for the same dependency across members. This is a known uv limitation that significantly affects GPU dependency management. The workaround (single index + optional extras + conflicts declaration) is now documented in architecture.md. This knowledge should be preserved for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0] describes the uv conflicting-index limitation. .factory/library/architecture.md 'GPU Dependency Management' section documents the workaround.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill's GPU dependency step says to use per-member sub-indexes ('01_data_filtering: ROCm torch, 02_pretraining: CUDA torch') but this is not feasible in uv workspaces. The skill's expected procedure doesn't account for the uv limitation. The skill should be updated to reflect the actual approach (optional extras + single index + conflicts).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 3 lists per-member GPU deps with separate index URLs, but the worker's deviation (documented in skillFeedback) shows this approach causes 'conflicting indexes error'. The actual working approach uses optional-dependencies + conflicts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md still lists the original per-member index URLs as dependency convention: 'ROCm modules: torch with --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/rocm6.2' and 'CUDA modules: torch with --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu124'. This no longer matches the actual implementation which uses a single generic index. Future workers following AGENTS.md literally would attempt the conflicting-index approach.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md 'Dependencies' section: 'ROCm modules: torch with --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/rocm6.2' — but the actual implementation uses optional-dependencies with a single index.

summary: The workspace-member-pyprojects feature is well-implemented and passes all validation contract assertions (VAL-WORKSPACE-001, VAL-WORKSPACE-006, VAL-WORKSPACE-008). All 4 member directories have correct pyproject.toml files with proper hardware isolation, each has src/<package>/__init__.py, the HF Hub transport module provides both required functions, and 25 smoke tests cover the workspace thoroughly. The worker's deviation from the skill procedure was justified by a uv workspace limitation. Two follow-up commits fixed Python 3.10 compatibility. Four shared state observations noted: (1) services.yaml install commands are incomplete, (2) uv limitation knowledge is well-documented, (3) skill procedure needs updating, (4) AGENTS.md dependency conventions are stale.
